title: CSI CUU / CUD / CUF / CUB — Relative Cursor Moves
summary: The four relative cursor-move commands shift the cursor by Pn cells (default 1) in one direction: CUU (final 'A') up, CUD ('B') down, CUF ('C') forward/right, CUB ('D') back/left. They do not scroll and stop at the screen edge.

attribution.note: xterm ctlseqs: CSI Ps A = Cursor Up Ps (CUU); CSI Ps B = Cursor Down (CUD); CSI Ps C = Cursor Forward (CUF); CSI Ps D = Cursor Backward (CUB). Default Ps = 1.
ECMA-48 defines CUU/CUD/CUF/CUB with a single Pn parameter defaulting to 1.

frame.note: CSI + single Pn + final byte: 'A' up, 'B' down, 'C' forward, 'D' back. Pn defaults to 1.

terminator_detail.note: No string terminator; the CSI sequence ends at its final byte. Parameters Ps are decimal, ';' (0x3B) separated.

params.name: CUU — Cursor Up (A)
params.meaning: CSI Pn A moves the cursor up Pn rows (default 1). Stops at the top of the page; does not scroll.

params.name: CUD — Cursor Down (B)
params.meaning: CSI Pn B moves the cursor down Pn rows (default 1).

params.name: CUF — Cursor Forward (C)
params.meaning: CSI Pn C moves the cursor right Pn columns (default 1).

params.name: CUB — Cursor Back (D)
params.meaning: CSI Pn D moves the cursor left Pn columns (default 1).

gotchas: A Pn of 0 is treated as 1, not as a no-op, per ECMA-48 default-parameter rules.
These moves CLAMP at the screen edges and never scroll the buffer — to scroll, use SU/SD or move past the scroll region's bottom with newlines.
CUF past the last column does not wrap to the next line; the cursor sticks at the right margin.
Do not confuse CUD ('B') with index/newline: CUD never scrolls, whereas LF/IND can scroll at the bottom margin.
'CSI D' (CUB, back) is distinct from the C0 BS (0x08) backspace though both move left one cell.

v1_smoke_test.asserts: CUU/CUD/CUF/CUB render as byte-exact single-parameter CSI sequences with final bytes A/B/C/D.
